core_mechanism: hypoelliptic regularity propagation from a bracket-generating (iterated-commutator) condition: a degenerate second-order operator built from a family of generating fields forces its solutions to automatically inherit smoothness/regularity because repeated Lie-bracket commutators of those fields span every direction of the space
brief_reason: The abstract studies the regularity of solutions of Hörmander's class of hypoelliptic operators, i.e. the machinery by which a degenerate (non-elliptic) operator nonetheless forces its solutions to be regular, whose hallmark is Hörmander's bracket condition guaranteeing hypoellipticity.
